summary: Completed read-only audit of DinoBrain durable source/provenance/source-chunk-claim lineage and graph health. Findings: graph health is correctly degraded but reports all accepted instances as missing source mapping; current source/provenance records exist for RAG guidance, but accepted-memory lineage recognition only checks narrow single-source fields and misses source_paths/source_chunk_path/claim_paths/provenance records. The graph index currently models record/root/folder/tag/kind/wikilink edges, not the provenance/lifecycle/quarantine/usage edges required by the completion contract. Node/npm were unavailable in the shell, so verification was static plus direct file inspection rather than npm verifier execution.
decisions: Kept repository source read-only; no code or data files were manually edited by the audit.
Used DinoBrain protocol context pack and primary RAG guidance sources for comparison.
Treated graph-health score 0/degraded as honest status, but incomplete lineage coverage remains a blocking graph-health issue.
next_steps: Teach graph-health/lifecycle/Observatory sourcePath logic to resolve source_paths arrays, source_chunk_path, provenance paths, and reverse claim_paths from .dino/provenance and 30_Sources/chunks.
Extend wiki-index graph edge types to include source/provenance, lifecycle/quarantine, and context-pack/task usage edges; then make graph health fail when required edge classes are absent.
Add a real-vault graph-health verifier alongside the current synthetic verifier and assert accepted/source/provenance counts, timestamp freshness, and lineage-edge coverage.
